violations.text: The presence of (rodent activity) is not controlled as evidenced by (rodent droppings). Per interview of staff storage areas in basement was cleaned. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Establishment shall monitor for pest activity by routinely cleaning areas. -Proof of pest control service must be provided to inspector. -A full evaluation by pest control must be performed before the next inspection. The full evaluation must include frequency of treatment, types of bait, traps, recommended preparations, structural findings, and technician assessment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

The walls in the (3-bay sink) area is constructed with nondurable materials. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

Observed (chemical spray bottles) stored on hand sink. Staff relocated spray bottles to designated area at time of inspection. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

Rodent droppings observed in basement underneath storage areas. Establishment shall clean droppings to monitor for any new activity. PIC to contact professional pest control to service establishment and provide pcs report to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

The walls in the (3-bay sink) area is constructed nondurable materials. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2026

At the time of inspection, sausage and bacon were observed stored in the Reach-In Cooler at 44 degrees Fahrenheit with an ambient temperature of the cooler at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Per interview with employee, fresh made hot tea was placed in the cooler to cool directly under the sausage and bacon. PIC removed the tea to cool outside of refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

Chemical spray bottles were observed stored hanging from the splash guard at the hand sink. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Spray bottles were moved to the floor below at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay. At the time of inspection, an employee obtained Bleach for sanitizing. Sanitizer was properly set up and tested at 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024
